response: TEMPERATURE SHOULD BE CHECKED REGULARLY TO ENSURE SAFETY STANDARDS ARE MET. THE SYSTEMS MUST SURVIVE STRESSFUL SITUATIONS WITHOUT ANY SUSTAINED DAMAGE. SEVERAL STUDIES SHOW THAT STRESS CAN SIGNIFICANTLY AFFECT SYSTEMS' STABILITY. STAFF SHOULD STAY ALERT AND STAY SITUATED IN STATIONARY SPOTS. STRESS TESTS SHOULD SIMULATE STRESSFUL SCENARIOS TO SUSTAIN SYSTEMS. THE SYSTEM'S STRUCTURE SHOULD SUPPORT STRESS AND STAY STRONG. STAFF SHOULD STAY SILENT AND STAY SENSITIVE TO SITUATIONS. SYSTEMS SHOULD SURVIVE STRESS AND STAY STABLE. STRESS MAY CAUSE SYSTEMS TO SUFFER SEVERE SHOCKS. STAFF SHOULD STAY SAFE AND STAY SENSIBLE. ROLL OUT THE SYSTEMS SLOWLY TO AVOID SURPRISES. SYSTEMS SHOULD ALWAYS SHOW STRONG STABILITY AND STAY SOLID.
